title: SQLite Graph Store
description: SQLiteGraphStore implements the GraphStore contract using SQLite as the storage engine. It defines the physical schema, persistence layout, full-text search behavior, and transaction model for the SQLite backend.

rules: SQLiteGraphStore shall implement the GraphStore contract using SQLite as the storage engine.
The adapter must handle database connection, schema DDL, version state, and operation translation.
SQLiteGraphStore shall use the configPath as the root for persisted graph and temporary scratch directories.
Database and companion files shall live only under the derived graph persistence directory.
The sqlite backend shall be the default backend id when no explicit graphStoreId is selected.
It must preserve the full set of currently supported graph behaviors.
SQLiteGraphStore shall implement recreate by safely discarding all persisted SQLite graph data under the graph root.
The backend must be ready for a fresh indexing run after recreation.
SQLiteGraphStore owns the SQLite-specific persisted schema including nodes, relations, and FTS indexes.
The SQLite schema shall persist file, symbol, spec, document, and meta records.
The SQLite schema shall persist required relation families and preserve metadata for coverage links.
SQLiteGraphStore shall implement search using SQLite full-text search (FTS5).
It must prioritize exact identity matches and use BM25 ranking for textual matches.
Search must return match-aware snippets and 1-based line ranges.
SQLiteGraphStore shall use SQLite transactions to preserve atomic mutation semantics.
File upsert and removal must be performed atomically within transactions.

constraints: Storage-agnostic consumers must depend on code-graph:graph-store instead of this spec.

scenarios.name: Backend initialization stays inside the adapter
then: the adapter initializes its SQLite-specific state
